Question: Use the method of separation of variables to find a general solution to the differential equation $$y \prime = 2 x y + 3 y - 4 x - 6 .$$
Find the solution to the initial-value problem $$6 y \prime = ( 2 x + 1 ) ( y^{2} - 2 y - 8 ) , y ( 0 ) = -3$$ using the method of separation of variables.

A tank contains $$3$$ kilograms of salt dissolved in $$75$$ liters of water. A salt solution of $$0.4 \text{kg salt/L}$$ is pumped into the tank at a rate of $$6 \text{L/min}$$ and is drained at the same rate. Solve for the salt concentration at time $$t .$$ Assume the tank is well mixed at all times.
Answer: Initial value problem: $$\frac{d u}{d t} = 2.4 - \frac{2 u}{25} , u ( 0 ) = 3$$ $$\text{Solution:} u ( t ) = 30 - 27 e^{\text{unicode[:-]} 2 t \text{/} 25}$$ $$\text{Concentration}:\textrm{ } 30 - 27 e^{\frac{- 2 \mathit{t}}{25}}$$

Question: A cake is removed from the oven after baking thoroughly, and the temperature of the cake when it comes out of the oven is $$450 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$ The temperature of the kitchen is $$70 \text{°} \text{F} ,$$ and after $$10$$ minutes the temperature of the cake is $$330 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$ Write the appropriate initial-value problem to describe this situation. Solve the initial-value problem for $$T ( t ) .$$ How long will it take until the temperature of the cake is within $$5 \text{°} \text{F}$$ of room temperature?
Answer: Initial value problem $$\frac{d T}{d t} = k ( T - 70 ) , T ( 0 ) = 450$$ $$T ( t ) = 70 + 380 e^{k t}$$ Approximately $$114$$ minutes.
